Canadian Utilities A ( (TSE:CU) ) has shared an update.

Canadian Utilities Limited plans to release its financial results for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, on May 6, 2026, with the full financial statements and management discussion to be posted on its website. The timing and format underscore the company’s regular disclosure practices and provide investors with an opportunity to evaluate performance across its diversified energy infrastructure businesses.

Management will host a teleconference and webcast that morning, led by Chief Executive Officer Bob Myles and Executive Vice President, Chief Financial & Investment Officer Katie Patrick, followed by a question-and-answer session with analysts. The call, accompanied by a replay and live webcast option, is designed to facilitate broad access for investors and analysts, highlighting the company’s emphasis on engagement with capital markets and transparency around its operational and financial outlook.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:CU) stock is a Hold with a C$47.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Canadian Utilities A stock, see the TSE:CU Stock Forecast page.

These are partly offset by strong technical momentum and a generally constructive earnings call focused on capital plan-driven regulated growth and improved operating cash flow, albeit with renewables and execution/funding risks.

To see Spark’s full report on CU stock, click here.

More about Canadian Utilities A

Canadian Utilities Limited, an ATCO company, is a diversified global energy infrastructure corporation with about 8,600 employees and $25 billion in assets. Through ATCO Energy Systems, ATCO EnPower and ATCO Australia, it delivers electricity and natural gas transmission and distribution, sustainable generation and storage, industrial water, cleaner fuels and other energy and infrastructure services across multiple regions.

data.title: Gemdale Gold Names New Corporate Secretary as It Advances Finnish Exploration Portfolio
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Gemdale Gold, Inc ( (TSE:GEMG) ) just unveiled an update.

Gemdale Gold Inc. has appointed corporate governance specialist Andrew Newbury as Corporate Secretary, succeeding Dr. Toby Strauss, who continues as President and CEO. Newbury brings more than 15 years of corporate secretarial, administrative, financial, and operational experience, advising small- and mid-cap issuers across major Canadian exchanges.

The company continues to advance a suite of Finnish exploration assets, including its Pontio, Isoneva, Lapland, Kumiseva, and Savo/Rantasalmi projects. Historical resource work, particularly at Savo/Rantasalmi, underpins Gemdale’s view of the portfolio’s potential, supporting its strategic focus on discovering new gold and critical metal deposits in a recognized European mining jurisdiction.

More about Gemdale Gold, Inc

Gemdale Gold Inc. is a mineral exploration company focused on gold and critical metals, holding a portfolio of highly prospective exploration licenses in Finland. Its 100% owned projects span Western, Northern, and Southeastern Finland, targeting gold, copper, nickel, platinum, and palladium in regions with established exploration activity and historical resource estimates.

data.title: EuroGroup Laminations Publishes Key Governance and Annual Reports Ahead of 2026 Shareholders’ Meeting
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from EuroGroup Laminations S.p.A ( (IT:EGLA) ).

EuroGroup Laminations has published key governance and reporting documents ahead of its ordinary shareholders’ meeting scheduled for 4 May 2026, making them available at its headquarters, on its website and via the 1info storage platform. The package includes the 2025 Integrated Annual Report, the 2025 corporate governance and ownership structure report, and the 2026 remuneration policy with the 2025 remuneration report.

The release underscores the company’s emphasis on transparency in financial, governance and pay practices at a time when it is scaling globally in e-mobility and industrial applications. By providing detailed disclosure on performance, ownership and executive compensation, EuroGroup Laminations aims to strengthen investor confidence and meet growing regulatory and stakeholder expectations on corporate governance standards.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:EGLA) stock is a Hold with a EUR1.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on EuroGroup Laminations S.p.A stock, see the IT:EGLA Stock Forecast page.

More about EuroGroup Laminations S.p.A

EuroGroup Laminations S.p.A. is a global leader in the design, production and distribution of stators and rotors for electric motors and generators, serving both e-mobility and industrial applications. Headquartered in Baranzate, near Milan, it operates 15 production plants worldwide, generated around €831 million in 2025 revenues, and supports sectors from electric vehicle traction to HVAC, wind power and transformers.

The group’s E-mobility solutions unit focuses on motor cores for electric vehicle traction and non-traction automotive uses, backed by an order backlog worth about €2.7 billion and a pipeline of €2.1 billion. Its Industrial & Infrastructure solutions unit supplies components for industrial machinery, home automation, logistics and pump systems, supported by a workforce of roughly 3,000 employees across Europe, the Americas, Asia and North Africa.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: €182.2M

data.title: Gold Hart Copper to Acquire Rhyolite Resources in All-Share Deal
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rhyolite Resources ( (TSE:RYE) ) has provided an update.

Gold Hart Copper Corp. has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire all outstanding shares of Rhyolite Resources Limited in an all-share deal, offering one Gold Hart share for every 2.6 Rhyolite shares. The transaction follows Gold Hart’s oversubscribed $18.6 million financing, positioning the combined company with strengthened funding for exploration across Chilean and other mineral properties.

Rhyolite’s board has unanimously approved the deal, backed by an independent fairness opinion, and is urging shareholders to vote in favour at a June 9 meeting. The merger, targeted to close in late Q2 2026 pending shareholder, court, regulatory approvals and working capital conditions, is expected to consolidate significant exploration assets and could enhance scale and strategic positioning in the copper-gold-silver exploration space.

More about Rhyolite Resources

Rhyolite Resources is a Vancouver-based mineral exploration company focused on acquiring, exploring and developing mineral properties. Gold Hart Copper is a Chile-focused exploration company and one of the largest independent landowners around major miners in the Maricunga Gold Belt and emerging Vicuña Copper Belt, targeting gold, copper and silver assets adjacent to some of the continent’s largest deposits.

Average Trading Volume: 195,956

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: C$12.11M

data.title: Caring Brands Faces Nasdaq Delisting After Equity Shortfall
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Caring Brands Incorporation ( (CABR) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 7, 2026, Caring Brands, Inc. disclosed that it received a Staff Delisting Determination from Nasdaq after its 2025 Form 10-K showed stockholders’ equity of $2.09 million, below the $2.5 million minimum required, and it also failed to meet Nasdaq’s alternative listing standards. Nasdaq has given the company until May 22, 2026, to submit a remediation plan and may allow up to October 4, 2026, to regain compliance, during which time CABR shares will remain listed, but investors face the risk of eventual delisting if compliance is not restored.

The company said it intends to submit a plan and take reasonable measures to remain on Nasdaq, yet it cautioned there is no assurance the plan will be accepted or that compliance can be achieved within the allowed time frame, leaving the listing status contingent on both Nasdaq’s discretion and the company’s financial performance.

More about Caring Brands Incorporation

Caring Brands, Inc. operates as a publicly traded company listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “CABR.” The company is subject to Nasdaq’s continued listing standards, including minimum stockholders’ equity thresholds and alternative financial criteria related to market value and net income performance.

Average Trading Volume: 132,477

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $14.44M

For an in-depth examination of CABR stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Humana ( (HUM) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 10, 2026, Humana Inc.’s Board of Directors expanded its size from ten to eleven members and elected Robert S. Field as an independent director, with his appointment to take effect immediately following the company’s 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders on April 16, 2026. Field will receive compensation under Humana’s standard director program, including restricted stock units, and there are currently no related-party transactions or special arrangements tied to his election.

Field is Principal and Managing Member of ηMed Capital Management, an investment firm focused on U.S. healthcare services and managed care, and he brings more than two decades of healthcare investing experience, including roles at Luxor Capital, McKinsey & Company and Vinson & Elkins LLP. Humana’s chairman highlighted that Field’s expertise in investment evaluation, risk oversight, capital allocation and healthcare legal and regulatory dynamics is expected to strengthen the board as the company executes its growth strategy, with his selection following a comprehensive, shareholder-informed search process underscoring Humana’s emphasis on board refreshment and corporate governance.

The most recent analyst rating on (HUM) stock is a Buy with a $211.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Humana stock, see the HUM Stock Forecast page.

The score is held back primarily by weakened profitability and a sharp drop in recent free cash flow, reinforced by cautious 2026 guidance tied to the ~$3.5B STARS headwind and near-term MA margin pressure. More about Humana

Humana Inc. is a leading U.S. healthcare company that operates through its Humana insurance services and CenterWell healthcare services, aiming to make it easier for millions of people to achieve their best health. The company delivers care and services to people with Medicare and Medicaid, as well as families, individuals, military service personnel and broader communities across the country.

Humana’s offerings span health insurance and direct healthcare services, positioning it as a major player in managed care and related healthcare delivery markets. Its strategy centers on improving quality of life while pursuing profitable, sustainable growth and long-term shareholder value in a highly regulated and competitive healthcare landscape.

data.title: Cherry Hill Mortgage Adopts New 2026 Executive Pay Plan
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Cherry Hill Mortgage ( (CHMI) ).

On April 6, 2026, Cherry Hill Mortgage’s board approved a new 2026 Executive Compensation Plan, effective January 1, 2026, for its top executives, following a comprehensive post-internalization review led by the compensation committee and an external consultant. The plan introduces a three-part structure—base salary, a short-term cash incentive tied chiefly to earnings available for distribution return on equity and relative valuation metrics, and a long-term equity program split between time-based and performance-based awards linked to relative and absolute total shareholder return, creating a heavily performance-weighted pay mix that aligns management rewards with shareholder outcomes and market competitiveness.

For 2026, the board set base salaries of $900,000 for CEO Jay Lown, $550,000 for CIO Julian Evans, and $400,000 for CFO Apeksha Patel, with STIP bonus opportunities ranging from 20% to 75% of salary depending on role and performance against financial and individual goals. The LTIP uses rolling three-year performance periods starting 2026–2028 and offers substantial upside for outperforming peers and absolute return targets, bringing total potential 2026 compensation to ranges of $1.76 million–$2.79 million for Lown, $1.10 million–$1.79 million for Evans, and $800,000–$1.30 million for Patel, underscoring the company’s effort to attract, retain, and motivate executives in line with shareholder returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (CHMI) stock is a Hold with a $2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cherry Hill Mortgage stock, see the CHMI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CHMI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CHMI is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak cash-flow quality and historically volatile results, alongside bearish technicals (sub-40 RSI and trading below key moving averages). Offsetting factors are a very high dividend yield and a cautiously positive earnings update with improving book value, strong liquidity, and prudent leverage, though mortgage/prepayment and mark-to-market risks remain meaningful.

To see Spark’s full report on CHMI stock, click here.

More about Cherry Hill Mortgage

Cherry Hill Mortgage Investment Corporation is a publicly traded real estate investment trust focused on residential mortgage assets. The company invests in a portfolio of mortgage-related assets and, following its transition to an internal management structure in November 2024, benchmarks its executive compensation against similarly sized, mortgage-focused public REITs.

Average Trading Volume: 198,192

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $98.09M

data.title: Cypherpunk Technologies Announces Planned Board Leadership Transitions
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Cypherpunk Technologies ( (CYPH) ) is now available.

On April 7, 2026, Cypherpunk Technologies said Class III directors Dr. Christopher Mirabelli and Dr. Joseph Loscalzo notified the company of their plans to retire from the Board and not stand for re‑election at the 2026 Annual Meeting, submitting resignations effective immediately before that meeting. The same day, Class I directors Dr. Richard Schilsky and Dr. James Cavanaugh also disclosed plans to retire effective immediately prior to the 2026 Annual Meeting, with the company emphasizing that none of the departures stemmed from disagreements over operations, policies or practices, signaling an orderly leadership transition in its boardroom.

The Board noted that Drs. Cavanaugh, Loscalzo, Mirabelli and Schilsky will also step down from their committee roles at the time of retirement tied to the 2026 Annual Meeting. By framing the exits as amicable and expressing gratitude for the directors’ years of service, Cypherpunk Technologies presented the moves as a planned refresh of its governance rather than a response to internal conflict, an important distinction for investors monitoring board stability and strategic continuity.

The most recent analyst rating on (CYPH) stock is a Hold with a $0.64 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cypherpunk Technologies stock, see the CYPH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CYPH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CYPH is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak operating fundamentals (near-zero revenue, ongoing operating losses, and continued cash burn), despite a notably strong low-debt balance sheet. Additional pressure comes from the Nasdaq minimum bid compliance risk, while valuation is not supportive due to a negative P/E and no provided dividend yield; technicals are neutral due to missing indicators.

To see Spark’s full report on CYPH stock, click here.

More about Cypherpunk Technologies

Cypherpunk Technologies Inc. operates in the technology sector, though this disclosure focuses on its corporate governance rather than its specific products or services. The company is publicly held and overseen by a Board of Directors structured in classes, reflecting a staggered governance model designed to provide continuity of oversight for shareholders and management.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell
